meta.title: Animesh Pandey · Senior Frontend Engineer | React, TypeScript, Next.js
meta.description: Animesh Pandey is a Senior Frontend Engineer based in Bangalore with 7+ years building scalable SaaS platforms: MMM dashboards, microfrontends for 50k+ daily users, and agentic AI products. Open to senior & staff roles.

hero.sub: Senior Frontend Engineer · React, TypeScript, Next.js
hero.tagline: I ship interfaces that survive real data: marketing intelligence dashboards, microfrontend platforms for 50k+ daily users, and agentic AI products. Based in Bangalore · open to remote-friendly roles.

skills.label: // skills
skills.title: Stack & craft
skills.intro: Production tools from recent platforms - not a buzzword dump.

writing.label: // writing
writing.title: On the craft
writing.intro: I write about the JavaScript patterns, system design decisions, and frontend engineering trade-offs I encounter at work - the stuff that doesn't make it into the docs.
writing.pipelineNote: Essays in progress — topics from production frontend engineering.
writing.pipelineFooter: Want early access? Say hi - happy to share drafts with engineers evaluating similar problems.

about.p1: I'm a Senior Frontend Engineer based in Bangalore, India, with 7+ years of experience building scalable SaaS platforms across analytics, automotive retail, and geospatial domains.
about.p2: At Lifesight, I lead frontend development for a unified marketing intelligence platform, building the dashboards and AI-powered interfaces that help enterprise brands make smarter decisions. Before that, I spent 4+ years at Tekion scaling frontend systems used by 50,000+ daily users.
about.p3: I believe great frontend engineering lives at the intersection of technical rigour and product empathy. I care about the milliseconds, the accessibility, the test coverage, not just the code that ships.

lifesight.desc: Leading frontend development for Lifesight's Unified Measurement OS, a marketing intelligence SaaS combining MMM, incrementality testing, and causal attribution. Building SSR analytics dashboards, contributing to Mia (their agentic AI product), and establishing platform-wide testing and performance standards.

tekion.desc: Owned 10+ mission-critical frontend modules across enterprise automotive retail. Built Storybook-documented component libraries and microfrontend architectures with Webpack Module Federation. Reduced load times by 30% for 50k+ daily users.

tekionAssociate.desc: Developed enterprise automotive retail applications using ReactJS, Redux, and React Native. Built 20+ reusable UI components and microfrontend modules documented in Storybook, accelerating feature delivery across engineering teams.

vassar.desc: Led frontend development of Kerala-WRIS dashboards providing real-time water resource visibility to millions of residents. Built geospatial visualisation systems for government clients and reduced API overhead by 30% through frontend optimisation.

vassarIntern.desc: Developed a real-time SCADA monitoring system for Andhra Pradesh Water Resources using AngularJS and D3.js, enabling operational dashboards for field teams.

projects.intro: Selected work across SaaS analytics, automotive retail, and GovTech.

microfrontend.title: Microfrontend Architecture at Scale
microfrontend.desc: Architected and shipped a production microfrontend system using Webpack Module Federation across 10+ mission-critical enterprise automotive retail modules, each owned by an independent squad. Achieved a 30% reduction in load times through aggressive code splitting and Lighthouse budgets in CI. The platform serves 50,000+ daily active users across dealership and OEM workflows.
microfrontend.metric: 50k+ daily users · 30% faster loads · 10+ independent teams

wris.title: Kerala-WRIS Water Intelligence Platform
wris.desc: Led frontend development for the Government of Kerala's statewide water resource information system. Built real-time geospatial dashboards over Leaflet with district-level overlays, time-series hydrology charts in Highcharts, and D3.js data-layer visualisations. Reduced API overhead by 30% through frontend caching and request batching strategies.
wris.metric: 30% API reduction · millions of residents served

mia.title: Mia: Agentic AI Analytics Interface
mia.desc: Built the frontend for Lifesight's agentic AI assistant, which interprets complex marketing datasets and surfaces attribution insights through a streaming conversational interface. Implemented real-time LLM response streaming, tool-call visualisation panels, and deep integration with the Unified Measurement OS data layer.
mia.metric: Agentic AI · Streaming UI · Tool-call visualisation

anomaly.title: Attribution Anomaly Intelligence
anomaly.desc: Embedded AI layer within the Lifesight attribution dashboard that continuously monitors media spend signals and proactively surfaces anomalies. Statistical changepoint detection runs on the data pipeline; results are handed to an LLM that generates plain-English explanations of what shifted and what action to consider.
anomaly.metric: AI anomaly detection · LLM narration · Proactive insights

marketingDash.title: Marketing Intelligence Dashboard
marketingDash.desc: Delivered enterprise-grade analytics dashboards powering Lifesight's Unified Measurement OS: MMM curves, incrementality test results, and causal attribution breakdowns for enterprise brands. Built with Next.js SSR for fast first-paint, enforced Core Web Vitals budgets in CI, and established platform-wide Playwright testing standards.
marketingDash.metric: SSR · Core Web Vitals · Playwright coverage

componentLib.title: Enterprise Component Library
componentLib.desc: Designed and maintained a Storybook-documented shared component library used across 10+ product modules at Tekion. Built 20+ reusable, accessible UI components with full TypeScript typing, WCAG 2.1 compliance, and interactive documentation. Reduced per-team duplicate code by 25%.

aiTooling.title: AI Dev Tooling Suite
aiTooling.desc: Internal CLI and VS Code extension that automated the highest-toil parts of Tekion's frontend development cycle, cutting team overhead by 30%. The scaffolder generates a fully wired microfrontend module from a single command. An AI-assisted PR review workflow flags accessibility regressions and performance anti-patterns before human review.

dealerApp.title: Dealer Operations Mobile App
dealerApp.desc: React Native companion to Tekion's web-based dealer management platform, designed for dealership staff on the shop floor. Covers real-time inventory lookup with VIN scanning, live service queue visibility, and customer CRM access. Built offline-first with SQLite-backed local state that syncs over a conflict-free delta protocol.
dealerApp.metric: Offline-first · VIN scanning · Shared Redux state

perfGuard.title: Performance CI Guard
perfGuard.desc: GitHub Actions pipeline that runs Lighthouse CI against a Playwright-rendered preview deployment on every pull request, automatically blocking merges when Core Web Vitals or bundle size regresses. Posts a generated PR comment with before/after metric tables, a bundle diff, and a 30-day trend sparkline.
perfGuard.metric: 12 regressions caught in month 1 · full CI coverage

writing.casebookDesc: Scrollable frontend case studies — skeleton screens, perceived performance, streaming UIs. With interactive demos and reading levels for junior, mid, and staff engineers.

functionalJs.title: Fundamentals of Functional JavaScript
functionalJs.excerpt: What makes a function pure, why procedures aren't functions, and how declarative thinking transforms the way you write JavaScript. A primer on functional programming fundamentals.

knowThis.excerpt: JavaScript's this is a runtime binding determined by call-site context. This piece works through all four binding rules (new, explicit, implicit, default) in order of precedence.

streamingAgent.title: Streaming Agent UI Without the Chatbot Clipart
streamingAgent.excerpt: How to build a streaming AI agent UI that feels native to your product. SSE lifecycle, tool-call panels, AbortController, UX states for thinking / streaming / error - and why skipping the chat bubble is the right call.

hackerNoonThis.excerpt: An in-depth look at JavaScript's most misunderstood keyword: implicit, explicit, and new binding rules demystified with real examples.

perfBudgets.title: Building Performance Budgets at Scale
perfBudgets.excerpt: How we wired Lighthouse CI, bundle budgets, and PR comments so 10+ microfrontends couldn't regress Core Web Vitals silently.

mfeProduction.title: Microfrontends: Lessons from Three Years in Production
mfeProduction.excerpt: Module Federation worked until it didn't - versioning, shared dependencies, and the debugging stories nobody puts in the sales deck.

dataDense.title: Data-Dense Dashboards Without Cognitive Overload
dataDense.excerpt: Patterns from MMM and attribution UIs: hierarchy, defaults, and when not to add another chart.

react18.title: From React 16 to 18 Across a Monorepo in Days
react18.excerpt: The migration playbook we used at Tekion - codemods, feature flags, and the tests that caught what types missed.

portfolioUx.title: Designing Recruiter-First Portfolio UX
portfolioUx.excerpt: Why I built a briefing mode, what I'd do differently, and how static sites can still feel product-grade.

canvasVsDom.title: Canvas vs DOM for Marketing Hero Effects
canvasVsDom.excerpt: When particles earn their keep - and when prefers-reduced-motion should win.

staticSites.title: Static Sites That Feel Premium Without a Framework
staticSites.excerpt: Tokens, lazy features, and discipline - how this portfolio is structured and why.

education.heading: Where I studied
education.degree: B.Tech in Computer Science and Engineering

items.aHtml: Yes, currently open to full-time senior or staff frontend engineering roles and consulting engagements. Based in Bangalore, open to remote globally. Reach me at animeshpandey1909@gmail.com or LinkedIn.

items.q: What technologies does Animesh specialise in?
items.aHtml: React, TypeScript, Next.js, Webpack Module Federation (microfrontends), design systems, SSR, performance optimisation, accessibility (WCAG 2.1), Playwright/Jest testing, and CI/CD with GitHub Actions.
Senior Software Engineer at Lifesight, a marketing intelligence SaaS in Bangalore, where he leads frontend for the Unified Measurement OS platform.
